subject: Leaf Pickup Schedule — October 2024
body_clean: Good morning, Bellwood residents. We're kicking off our fall leaf pickup program this Monday, October 7th. Crews will work block by block through the village over the following four weeks — your zone schedule is posted at bellwoodil.gov/leafpickup. Please rake leaves to the parkway strip by 7:00 a.m. on your collection day and keep them free of branches and bagged garbage. We ask for your patience as crews navigate high-volume streets first. Any questions, call Public Works at 708-555-0142. Thank you for keeping Bellwood clean.

subject: Snow Route Parking Reminder — January 2025
body_clean: Residents, with a winter storm watch in effect beginning tonight, please remember that all designated snow routes in Bellwood must be clear of parked vehicles when two or more inches of snow are on the ground or actively falling. Snow route signs are posted at each block entrance. Vehicles left on a snow route may be ticketed and towed at owner's expense. A full map of snow routes is available on the village website. If you have a question about whether your street qualifies, please call the Police non-emergency line at 708-555-0110.

subject: Taste of Bellwood 2025 — This Weekend!
body_clean: Bellwood, we're counting down the hours! Taste of Bellwood opens Friday, July 11th at 5 p.m. on Washington Blvd between 25th and 32nd Avenues. We have 18 food vendors, two stages of live music, carnival rides, and a fireworks display Saturday night at 9:30. Free admission — parking is available at Village Hall and the Metra station lot. St. Charles Rd will be closed to through traffic from 24th to 33rd Avenues Friday afternoon through Sunday night; please plan your route accordingly. See you there — this is our community's weekend.

subject: Spring Cleanup Week: Large-Item Pickup April 22–26
body_clean: On behalf of Mayor Okonkwo, this is your reminder that Spring Cleanup Week runs April 22–26. Large items — furniture, appliances, exercise equipment, mattresses — may be placed at the curb the evening before your regular garbage day. Please do not set items out more than 24 hours early. Electronics must be taken to the Cook County household hazardous waste drop-off; they will not be collected curbside. Latex paint that is fully dried is acceptable. Questions? Contact the Sanitation Division at 708-555-0133.

subject: Hydrant Flushing Notice — February 2024
body_clean: Bellwood residents, Public Works crews will conduct their annual hydrant flushing program beginning Monday, February 5th, working through the end of the month street by street. During flushing in your area, you may notice reduced water pressure or temporary discoloration — this is normal and will clear within 30 to 60 minutes after running a cold tap. Please avoid doing laundry while flushing is active on your block if possible, as the sediment can stain light-colored fabrics. We apologize for the inconvenience; this work protects fire-suppression capacity and water quality throughout the village.

subject: Thank You for Reporting the Pothole on Marshall Ave
body_clean: Ms. Kowalski, thank you for taking the time to write. I passed your report about the pothole near 1815 Marshall Ave to Frank DiMeo and the Public Works crew this morning. Frank tells me it's on today's afternoon repair list. We know that intersection takes a beating from heavy truck traffic, and we're also looking at a longer-term mill-and-overlay for that block later this season. I appreciate residents like you who keep us honest — please don't hesitate to reach out again if the repair doesn't hold.

subject: Volunteer Sign-Ups Open for Taste of Bellwood 2025
body_clean: We're looking for community volunteers to help make Taste of Bellwood 2025 a success! Volunteer shifts run three to four hours and include roles in vendor check-in, information tables, litter patrol, and the children's activity tent. All volunteers receive a festival T-shirt and meal vouchers. Sign up at bellwoodil.gov/tastevolunteer or call the Parks office at 708-555-0155 by June 20th. Mayor Okonkwo personally thanks everyone who's given their time in past years — this festival wouldn't happen without you.

subject: Your Noise Complaint — St. Charles Rd, Fri. Sept. 12
body_clean: Ms. Pawlak, I hear you, and I want you to know this isn't falling through the cracks. Officer Banks reviewed the patrol log for Friday night and confirmed a second warning was issued to the establishment at 624 St. Charles at 12:40 a.m. I've asked him to ensure a patrol car makes a visible pass near your block on Friday and Saturday evenings for the next several weeks. I also had Sandra Pulaski check on the outdoor amplification condition in their operating license — she'll be following up directly. You deserve a decent night's sleep.

subject: Holiday Garbage Collection Schedule — January 2026
body_clean: Please note that garbage and recycling collection will be delayed by one day for the week of January 19th in observance of the Martin Luther King Jr. holiday. If your regular pickup day is Monday, January 19th, your collection will occur Tuesday, January 20th. All other days shift forward by one as well — Thursday and Friday customers should contact the sanitation office if they have questions. Place carts and bins at the curb by 6:00 a.m. on your adjusted collection day. Thank you for your patience.

subject: Pothole Repair Season Underway — Report Issues Online
body_clean: Spring is here, and our crews are out patching the winter damage across Bellwood. If you spot a pothole on a village-maintained street, please report it at bellwoodil.gov/report or call 708-555-0142 — the more specific you can be about the address and lane position, the faster we can get to it. Note that Mannheim Rd is an IDOT state route; for issues there, please report directly to IDOT at idot.illinois.gov. We're targeting the worst arterials first and will work through residential streets through May. Thank you for your patience this season.

subject: Bellwood Public Library Summer Reading Kickoff — June 8
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo is pleased to announce that this year's Summer Reading kickoff at the Bellwood Public Library will be held Saturday, June 8th from 10 a.m. to 1 p.m. The mayor will stop by at 10:30 to read to the kids. There will be free ice cream, craft stations, and a raffle for gift cards to local businesses. The library is located at 600 Bohland Ave. No registration required — all ages welcome. We hope to see families from across Bellwood join us for a wonderful start to summer.

subject: Thank You for Your Sidewalk Concern — Eastern Ave
body_clean: Ms. Coleman, thank you for the heads-up about the raised sidewalk panel near the 300 block of Eastern Ave. I know that's a real trip hazard, especially with strollers and little ones. Public Works flagged it this morning and it's been added to the November repair list — we should have it ground down or replaced before Thanksgiving. I really appreciate that you're keeping an eye on your block. If anything else comes up near you, please write anytime.

subject: Reminder: Clear Snow from Sidewalks Within 24 Hours
body_clean: With the season's first significant snowfall expected this week, the Office of the Mayor wants to remind all property owners that Bellwood ordinance requires sidewalks adjacent to your property to be cleared within 24 hours after snowfall ends. This includes property owners whose homes sit on corner lots — both sidewalks must be shoveled. Salt and sand are available free of charge to residents at the Public Works garage, 700 Bohland Ave, while supplies last. The village's senior snow-shoveling assistance program is also available; call 708-555-0198 to request help.

subject: Halloween Safety Tips and Trick-or-Treat Hours
body_clean: Happy almost-Halloween, Bellwood! This year's official trick-or-treat hours are Thursday, October 31st from 3:00 p.m. to 7:30 p.m. Motorists, please slow down and watch for children in costumes — especially after dusk. Parents, please review your children's candy before allowing them to eat it, and consider adding reflective tape or a glow stick to their costumes. Residents who would like to participate should leave a porch light on. Public Works has been asked to ensure all street lights are functioning on major trick-or-treat routes. Have a safe and fun night, everyone.

subject: Recycling Program Update — New Accepted Materials
body_clean: Beginning May 1st, Bellwood's curbside recycling program will expand to accept rigid plastic containers numbered 1 through 7 — previously only 1 and 2 were collected. Aluminum foil and clean pie tins will also now be accepted. As a reminder, plastic bags and film CANNOT go in the recycling cart and will cause contamination fines for the village. Please place bags in your regular garbage. Updated material lists are included in the spring village newsletter mailed this week and are posted at bellwoodil.gov/recycling.

subject: Missed Garbage Collection — Rice Ave, Aug. 14
body_clean: Mr. Delgado, I'm sorry your garbage was missed on Thursday — that shouldn't happen. I forwarded your note to the Sanitation Division and they confirmed your address was skipped in error. A crew will swing by tomorrow morning, Saturday the 16th, to collect. If for any reason the cart isn't emptied by noon, please call 708-555-0133 directly and reference your service confirmation number 2025-8142. I appreciate you letting us know promptly — it helps us track driver routes and catch these mistakes.

subject: Road Closures for Taste of Bellwood — June 27–29, 2026
body_clean: Heads up, Bellwood commuters: Washington Blvd between 25th Avenue and 32nd Avenue will be closed to vehicle traffic Friday, June 27th starting at noon through Sunday, June 29th at 10 p.m. for Taste of Bellwood. Detour signs will direct drivers via Madison St and Bohland Ave. The Metra UP-W station remains fully accessible via 25th Ave. Please allow extra time if you travel through this corridor on those days. Full detour maps are at bellwoodil.gov/tastedetour. We look forward to seeing you at the festival.

subject: Resurfacing Work Begins on Granville Ave — Sept. 23
body_clean: Neighbors along Granville Ave between Marshall Ave and 19th Ave: paving crews from Verdi Excavating & Drainage will begin milling and resurfacing your block Monday, September 23rd. Work is expected to run through Thursday the 26th, weather permitting. During those four days, on-street parking will be restricted to the west side only. Please plan for some noise and minor delays between 7 a.m. and 5 p.m. This project has been on our capital plan for two years and I'm glad we're finally getting it done. Thank you for your patience while crews are on the block.

subject: Hazardous Waste Collection Event — November 15, 2025
body_clean: Cook County and the Village of Bellwood are hosting a household hazardous waste drop-off event on Saturday, November 15th from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. in the Village Hall parking lot, 3200 Washington Blvd. Accepted materials include paints, solvents, pesticides, fluorescent bulbs, and batteries. This event is open to Bellwood residents only — please bring a water bill or other proof of residence. No commercial or contractor waste will be accepted. Quantities are limited per household. No appointment needed; arrive before 12:30 p.m. to ensure you can be processed before closing.

subject: Thank You for Reporting the Drainage Issue on Hirsch Ave
body_clean: Thank you for writing in about the standing water at the corner of Hirsch Ave and 50th Ave after last week's rain. I had Frank DiMeo's team out there on Tuesday and they found a partially blocked catch basin — roots had grown into the inlet. The crew cleared it Wednesday morning and we're scheduling a camera inspection of that lateral to make sure there isn't a bigger problem downstream. I know that intersection has been soggy for a while; I'm glad you flagged it so we could finally dig into the cause. I'll follow up once we have the inspection results.
